Defines the Number.prototype built-in object and its properties and routines for JerryScript.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines the properties and routines for the `Number.prototype` object in a JavaScript engine. It uses macros to declare object identifiers, object values, and routine properties associated with the `Number.prototype`. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `Number.prototype`, while the `OBJECT_VALUE` macro defines the constructor property with configurable and writable attributes. The `ROUTINE` macro specifies several methods such as `toString`, `valueOf`, `toLocaleString`, `toFixed`, `toExponential`, and `toPrecision`, each with its corresponding C function name, number of arguments, and length property value. This setup facilitates the integration of JavaScript's `Number.prototype` functionalities into the engine's C implementation.
